summary: Vercel builder package exfiltrates host identity and environment variables to hardcoded AWS API Gateway
details: The package implements the Vercel @vercel/build-utils builder contract (exports build, analyze, prepareCache) so it is invoked automatically by the Vercel build pipeline when consumed as a builder. Inside index.js, the build() function reads process.getuid(), os.hostname(), process.uptime(), and Vercel/AWS-specific environment variables (AWS_EXECUTION_ENV, VERCEL_CLUSTER, VERCEL_HIVE_INSTANCE_TYPE, VERCEL_REGION) and POSTs them to the hardcoded endpoint https://vu7l71ljr2.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/prod/api/read/p1216-internal-ssrf-proof. The path string suggests this is bug-bounty probe tooling, but any installer who consumes this builder unconditionally leaks internal infrastructure metadata to a third-party AWS API Gateway with no opt-in or disclosure. The package name and shape mimic a legitimate Vercel builder, increasing the chance of accidental adoption.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
